steps.title: Apply the angle sum property of triangles
steps.content:
The angles of any triangle sum to 180°:
$$\angle D + \angle E + \angle F = 180^{\circ}$$
Substituting the given value $\angle D = 114^{\circ}$:
$$114^{\circ} + \angle E + \angle F = 180^{\circ}$$
$$\angle E + \angle F = 66^{\circ}$$

steps.title: Determine which choice satisfies the constraint
steps.content:
For a valid triangle, both $\angle E$ and $\angle F$ must be positive angles. Since $\angle E + \angle F = 66^{\circ}$ and both must be positive, we need $0^{\circ} \lt \angle E \lt 66^{\circ}$.

Testing each choice:
  • A) $115^{\circ}$ — exceeds $66^{\circ}$, so $\angle F$ would be negative
  • B) $90^{\circ}$ — exceeds $66^{\circ}$, so $\angle F$ would be negative
  • C) $67^{\circ}$ — exceeds $66^{\circ}$, so $\angle F$ would be negative
  • D) $65^{\circ}$ — less than $66^{\circ}$, so $\angle F = 1^{\circ}$ (valid)
That confirms D is correct.
